article_title: OpenTitan Becomes the First Open-Source Silicon Root of Trust to Ship in Commercial Hardware as Google Targets Data Centers Next

content_preview.title: OpenTitan Becomes the First Open-Source Silicon Root of Trust to Ship in Commercial Hardware as Google Targets Data Centers Next
content_preview.summary: Google's seven-year open-source hardware security project reaches production in Dell Chromebooks with post-quantum cryptography, and data center deployment planned for later this year.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenTitan, the open-source silicon root of trust (RoT) project launched by Google and partners in 2019, has reached a defining milestone: it is now shipping inside commercially available Chromebooks. The achievement, [announced by the OpenTitan team](https://opensource.googleblog.com/2026/03/opentitan-shipping-in-production.html) on the Google Open Source Blog, makes OpenTitan the first open-source hardware RoT to reach production, and the first to support post-quantum cryptographic...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent technical article on a significant hardware security milestone. Clear explanation of RoT concepts, PQC, and community metrics. 733 words for News.
editor_notes.source_verification: Google Open Source Blog confirmed all key claims: Dell Chromebook models, Nuvoton, first open-source RoT, SLH-DSA PQC, Earl Grey/Darjeeling, 90%+ coverage, 40K nightly tests, 29.2K commits, 275 contributors, lowRISC, Apache 2.0, ownership transfer, ML-DSA/ML-KEM next gen, data center 2026. TechRadar supplementary for Pixel/data center context.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against Google's primary announcement. Technical details match precisely.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and informative. Good balance of technical depth and accessibility.
editor_notes.originality: No prior OpenTitan coverage. Novel topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality article with primary source verification. Ready for publication.
